pages.title: User Funnel | User Engagement
pages.key_finding: March saw User Stickiness artificially inflate to 14.28% as Monthly Active Users nearly halved (from 37 to 21), leaving only the most dedicated core players. While initial registration conversion remains strong at 87.7%, a staggering 55.4% of users still drop off before actually playing, underlining an urgent need for targeted re-engagement campaigns.

pages.title: Game Performance (Score)
pages.key_finding: Average daily scores continued to fall rapidly, dropping another 57.7% from 28,986 in February to 12,265 in March. This ongoing downward trend confirms that even the retained player base is becoming less active or less motivated to achieve high scores.

pages.title: Game Performance (Time)
pages.key_finding: Playtime experienced a parallel decline, falling 53.1% from 32 minutes per day in February to just 15 minutes in March. Without fresh content or engaging events, players are completing shorter sessions and logging off earlier than in previous months.
